title: hieracium segureum subsp. cirritiforme
intro: Hieracium segureum subsp. cirritiforme, also known as Mouse-ear Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, improving liver function, and fighting against certain bacterial infections. The herbal preparation of Hieracium segureum subsp. cirritiforme is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it.
